questions.text: Why is this animal called a turtle frog?
options: It is a turtle that eats frogs.
It is a frog that lives with turtles.
It is a frog that looks like a turtle.
It is a turtle that moves like a frog.

Turtle frogs use their strong legs to
Why is it unusual to see a turtle frog?
options: It often hides in its nest.
It is too small for people to see.

questions.text: What does the text tell us about termites?
options: They eat one meal a day.

questions.text: What do the illustrations show?
options: what the turtle frog looks like
how the turtle frog builds a nest
which leaves the turtle frog eats
where the turtle frog normally lives

questions.text: Pia wants a rabbit as a classroom pet. What is one reason she gives?
options: Rabbits are simple to care for.

questions.text: Who knows where to find a blue-tongue lizard?
Class 4F may not keep a blue-tongue lizard because
options: they do not have a permit.
their teacher does not like lizards.
it would be too cold in the classroom.

questions.text: Pia says, He means it's cruel when birds aren't free and can't fly around. Why does she say this?
What does Mr Finch do in this text?
options: He says what pet he wants.
He agrees with the students' ideas.
He lets the students share their ideas.
He tells the students about different pets.

questions.text: The text says a game of SPUD needs at least
The winner is the player who
options: is the first to spell SPUD.

questions.text: When the player who is It calls your name, what must you do first?
Which of these changes would make the game last longer?
options: spelling a word with more letters

questions.text: Which picture best matches Rule 5?
You could even play SPUD on a netball court. Where would this information belong in this text?
Who wrote the note?
Rosie’s note is shown in the text. This helps to
options: make the reader feel sorry for Rosie.
tell the reader that Rosie made up this story.
make the reader feel more part of Rosie’s story.
tell the reader something Rosie doesn’t know.

questions.text: The purpose of the meeting is to let children and parents know
options: who the music teacher will be.
who has been picked for the band.
what the band members will have to do.
what progress the band members have made.

questions.text: What is Rosie’s mum most likely to think about Rosie being in the band?
options: This will be easy for her.
I am sure she will be successful.
I think she is making a mistake.
She needs to think about this carefully.

questions.text: 'It certainly will be a commitment.' Why does Rosie’s mum say this?

When he first saw the Naadam festival horseraces, Angus felt
options: disappointed that the jockeys were children.
bored because the races took so long.
inspired to take part in the races.

questions.text: After he returned to Australia, ... The second paragraph is about Angus’s
Angus’s Mongolian friends helped him to
options: take part in the Mongolian races.

Which idea best matches this text?
options: There is no place like home.
Fight for what you believe in.
Good things come to those who wait.
Dreams can come true if you work hard.

questions.text: Why did Angus receive the Young Adventurer of the Year Award?
options: He did something to help others.

questions.text: What is another suitable title for this text?
options: The day we saw a platypus
Why the platypus has a flat tail

questions.text: In line 5 of the text, the arrow of water refers to
options: the shadows of the trees on the water.
the shape of the ripples on the water.
the sound of the flowing water.
the speed of the flowing water.

questions.text: What helped the children to see the platypus better?
options: It moved into a sunny place.
It climbed up onto the riverbank.
It put its head out of the water.

In this text, all of the characters show they are
Who is telling this story?
